questions.question: What is the shape of the Earth?
questions.explanation: The Earth is nearly a perfect sphere, but slightly flattened at the poles.

questions.question: What are parallels of latitude?
questions.correct_answer: Imaginary lines running east and west, parallel to the Equator
questions.explanation: Parallels of latitude measure distance from the Equator.

questions.question: What is the Prime Meridian?
questions.correct_answer: A reference point for measuring longitude
questions.explanation: It passes through Greenwich, London, and is marked as 0° longitude.

questions.question: How are longitudes measured?
questions.correct_answer: Distance from the Prime Meridian along the Equator
questions.explanation: Longitude increases in value from 0° to 180° west or east of the Prime Meridian.

questions.question: What is the difference between latitude and longitude?
questions.correct_answer: Latitude measures distance from the Equator, while longitude measures distance from the Prime Meridian
questions.explanation: Together, they provide coordinates for locating any place on Earth.

questions.question: Why can't a globe be flattened without tearing it?
questions.explanation: A sphere cannot be accurately represented on a flat surface.

questions.question: What is the significance of longitude in measuring time?
questions.correct_answer: Longitude measures the time at a place due to the Earth's rotation
questions.explanation: As you travel east or west, the local time changes.

questions.question: Why are latitude and longitude important?
questions.correct_answer: They allow for precise location of any place on Earth
questions.explanation: With these coordinates, you can pinpoint any location globally.

questions.question: What is the connection between latitude and climate?
questions.correct_answer: Latitude affects climate; around the Equator it's hot, while at poles it's cold
questions.explanation: Climate becomes more moderate as you move away from the Equator towards one of the poles.

questions.question: Why is a globe useful for representing Earth's geography?
questions.correct_answer: Because it accurately represents the spherical shape of the Earth
questions.explanation: A globe provides a better representation than a flat map.

important_notes.title: Latitude and Longitude Basics
important_notes.content: Latitude measures distance from the Equator, while longitude measures distance from the Prime Meridian. Together, they provide coordinates for locating any place on Earth.

important_notes.title: Earth's Shape and Globe Representation
important_notes.content: The Earth is nearly a perfect sphere, but slightly flattened at the poles. A globe accurately represents this spherical shape, making it useful for geography representation.

important_notes.title: Understanding Coordinates
important_notes.content: Coordinates are used to locate places on Earth using latitude and longitude values. These values help pinpoint any location globally.

important_notes.title: Latitude and Climate Relationship
important_notes.content: Latitude affects climate, with hotter temperatures near the Equator and colder temperatures closer to poles.

important_notes.title: Prime Meridian Importance
important_notes.content: The Prime Meridian serves as a reference point for measuring longitude. It's marked at 0° longitude and used internationally.

important_notes.title: Longitude Measurement
important_notes.content: Longitude measures distance from the Prime Meridian along the Equator, with values increasing from 0° to 180° west or east.

important_notes.title: Globe Features and Representation
important_notes.content: A globe represents Earth's geography accurately due to its spherical shape. It includes parallels of latitude and meridians of longitude for precise location.

important_notes.title: Earth's Rotation and Time Measurement
important_notes.content: The Earth rotates on its axis, causing different times at various locations. Longitude measures this time difference.

important_notes.title: Importance of Latitude and Longitude
important_notes.content: Latitude and longitude are crucial for navigation, mapping, and understanding global geography. They allow for precise location and measurement of distances.

important_notes.title: Earth's Spherical Shape and Globe Representation
important_notes.content: A sphere cannot be accurately represented on a flat surface due to its shape. A globe provides an accurate representation of Earth's geography.

facts.fact: The Equator is 0° latitude, while the North and South Poles are 90° N and 90° S respectively.
Latitude affects climate; around the Equator it's hot, while at poles it's cold.
The Prime Meridian is marked as 0° longitude and serves as a reference point for measuring longitude internationally.
Longitude measures distance from the Prime Meridian along the Equator, with values increasing from 0° to 180° west or east.
The Earth's rotation causes different times at various locations, and longitude measures this time difference.
A globe provides an accurate representation of Earth's geography due to its spherical shape.
Latitude and longitude are crucial for navigation, mapping, and understanding global geography.
The connection between latitude and climate affects the type of vegetation, temperature, and weather at different locations on Earth.
A globe is useful for representing Earth's geography because it accurately represents the spherical shape of the planet.
Latitude and longitude are essential tools for pilots, sailors, and travelers to navigate and locate places globally.
